meta.notes: Figure-read values (Figures 3-9) approximated from text descriptions; underlying numeric figure data not tabulated in main text.

claims.text: AF-XNBR gloves can achieve ASTM D6319-compliant performance even under low-temperature curing conditions.

location.quote:
AF-XNBR gloves can achieve compliant
performance even under low-temperature curing conditions

claims.predicate: measured tensile strength >= 14 MPa and elongation at break >= 500% at curing temperature 65 C
claims.scope: 55 um gloves, curing 65-110 C, ASTM D6319

claims.text: ASTM D6319-19 specifies a minimum tensile strength of 14 MPa and a minimum elongation at break of 500% for nitrile examination gloves.

location.section: 3.7 Tensile Properties and Tear Strength Test
location.quote:
which
specifies a minimum tensile strength of 14 MPa and a minimum
elongation at break of 500% for nitrile examination gloves.

claims.predicate: ASTM D6319-19 min_tensile_strength_MPa == 14 and min_elongation_pct == 500

claims.text: The latex compound pH was brought down to roughly 10 using a 5% KOH aqueous solution.

location.quote:
A 5% KOH aqueous solution was used to
bring the compound’s pH down to roughly 10.

claims.predicate: reported_ph == 10

claims.text: The swelling index of aged samples is consistently lower than that of unaged samples across all latex grades and curing temperatures, and this reduction is statistically significant (p < 0.05).

location.quote:
This reduction was found to
be statistically significant (p < 0.05), signifying that aging leads
to a measurable decrease in solvent uptake.

claims.predicate: reported p-value bound p_upper_bound == 0.05 for aged vs unaged swelling index comparison
claims.scope: 55 um gloves, all latex grades and curing temperatures

claims.text: XNBR gloves with K834 and NL129 show high swelling (~250-275%) when unaged, decreasing to ~210-230% after aging.

location.quote:
XNBR gloves with latex K834 and NL129 for all three
curing conditions show high swelling (∼250−275%) when
unaged. After aging, swelling decreases moderately (∼210−
230%)

claims.predicate: unaged swelling index in [250,275]% and aged swelling index in [210,230]% for K834 and NL129
claims.scope: K834 and NL129, all curing conditions

claims.text: XNBR gloves using NL117 show much lower initial swelling (~140-150%) and slight further reduction after aging (~125-135%).

location.quote:
XNBR gloves using latex NL117 show much lower
initial swelling (∼140−150%) compared to other latexes
throughout all curing conditions. Slight further reduction after
aging (∼125−135%)

claims.predicate: unaged NL117 swelling index in [140,150]% and aged in [125,135]%

claims.text: NL117 consistently exhibits the highest calculated cross-link density among latex grades.

location.quote: NL117 consistently exhibiting the highest values

claims.predicate: cross-link density of NL117 > cross-link density of K834 and NL129 across all conditions in Table 4

claims.text: K834 latex shows lower crotch retention after aging (~36-57%).

location.quote:
K834 latex shows lower retention after aging (crotch
retention: ∼ 36−57%)

claims.predicate: K834 crotch retention in [36,57]% (Table 6 values 35.8, 45.8, 56.7)

location.quote:
NL129 latex
has moderate-to-high retention (68−93%)

claims.predicate: NL129 crotch retention in [68,93]% (Table 6 values 68.3, 75.0, 92.5)

claims.text: NL117 latex performs best with crotch retention values approaching or exceeding 100%.

location.quote:
NL117 latex performs
the best, with retention values approaching or exceeding 100%.

claims.predicate: NL117 crotch retention >= ~95% (Table 6 values 95.8, 100.0, 102.6)

claims.text: K834 at 70 um tear strength increases from ~65 kN/m (unaged) to ~150 kN/m (aged).

location.quote:
K834 at 70 μm increases from ∼65 kN/m (unaged)
to ∼150 kN/m (aged)

claims.predicate: old_value ~65 kN/m, new_value ~150 kN/m tear strength for K834 at 70 um

claims.text: K834 at 90 um shows the highest tear strength (~160 kN/m aged).

location.quote:
K834 at 90 μm shows the highest values

claims.predicate: aged tear strength K834 90 um ~160 kN/m, highest in Figure 5

claims.text: Tensile strength of latex films cured at 65 C reached about 30 MPa, similar to 110 C; K834 shows consistently high tensile strength (~30 MPa).

location.quote:
the tensile
strength of the latex films cured at 65 °C reached about 30
MPa, which is similar to 110 °C. In particular, K834 latex
shows consistently high tensile strength across all curing
temperatures (∼30 MPa).

claims.predicate: tensile strength at 65 C ~30 MPa and ~similar at 110 C (Figure 6)

claims.text: All XNBR latex gloves regardless of grade and curing temperature demonstrated elongation at break exceeding 500%, with no significant differences (p > 0.05) among grades and temperatures.

location.quote:
demonstrated elongation at break values
exceeding 500%. Statistical analysis using an independent t test
confirmed that there were no significant differences (p > 0.05)
in elongation at break among the different latex grades and
curing temperatures.

claims.predicate: elongation at break > 500% for all conditions; t-test p_lower_bound == 0.05 (n.s.) among grades/temperatures
claims.scope: 55 um gloves, Figure 7, all grades and temperatures

claims.text: Aging resulted in a significant increase in tensile strength (p < 0.05) for all latex grades, while no significant differences (p > 0.05) were observed between latex grades within the same aging condition.

location.quote:
aging resulted in a
significant increase in tensile strength (p < 0.05) for all latex
grades, while no significant differences (p > 0.05) were
observed between the different latex grades within the same
aging condition.

claims.predicate: t-test p_upper_bound == 0.05 for aging effect on tensile strength; p_lower_bound == 0.05 among grades
claims.scope: 55 um gloves cured at 65 C, Figures 8 and 9
